decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a Bayesian Poisson framework to impute incomplete notification records and infer occupationally underreported leptospirosis burden.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to New Zealand leptospirosis notifications.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates unreported COVID-19 cases and deaths by comparing observed SARI counts with time-series estimates of expected counts.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete under-reporting estimation across Brazilian states.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Links hospital and mortality records to identify misclassified AIDS deaths and reconstruct the mortality rate after including underreported deaths.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Applied reconstruction of AIDS mortality in Brazil.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses direct and excess-mortality approaches to quantify under-reported COVID-19 deaths and adjust the estimated mortality burden.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Applied COVID-19 mortality reconstruction in Brazil.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes and validates a regularized inverse-problem approach using age-specific hospital admissions to reconstruct actual first-wave infections.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development for correcting infection underreporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Integrates official statistics, medical reports, clinical assessments, and press coverage to assess systematic underreporting and reconstruct poliomyelitis's broader societal burden.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Historical application to poliomyelitis burden in Turkey.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses laboratory testing, surgical specimens, and a community survey to identify cases and characterize an underreported typhoid outbreak.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete outbreak case-finding application in Uganda.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies rapid field epidemiology and a cohort study to identify cryptosporidiosis cases, estimate illness burden, locate the source, and limit spread.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete outbreak case-finding application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Validates an interactive mobile app's compliance and performance for detecting and ascertaining acute otitis media symptom episodes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit validation of a case-finding tool against conventional monitoring.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates milk as a novel alternative sample for targeted FMD surveillance and demonstrates its ability to detect and type FMD virus.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological evaluation of a novel surveillance sampling approach.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and evaluates machine-learning models that integrate NLP-derived and structured EHR features for surgical-site-infection identification.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of an enhanced electronic case-identification method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Provides a complete field protocol for decentralized postmortem rabies diagnosis, including sampling, rapid testing, interpretation, and downstream molecular analysis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit protocol implementation for field case confirmation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates feasibility and inter-rater concordance of a web-based tool for community detection of post-discharge surgical-site infections.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit reliability and feasibility assessment of the surveillance tool.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reviews point-of-care animal-disease tests and directly examines field-validation deficiencies, quality risks, and technical and implementation barriers.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Literature review supplies explicit implementation considerations and cautions for surveillance-enhancing diagnostics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Screens an unselected autopsy cohort for SARS-CoV-2 and other pathogens to uncover infections and deaths missed before autopsy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete enhanced postmortem case-finding application for COVID-19.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs retrospective serologic case finding among previously unexplained viral CNS infections to detect missed tick-borne encephalitis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete retrospective case-finding study in Sweden.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents a novel case-detection approach combining longitudinal serology with PCR or antigen testing and demonstrates substantially increased reinfection ascertainment.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development and comparison of an enhanced reinfection-detection approach.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Pilots community-based alerts combined with field diagnostic testing to confirm rabid dogs and alleviate underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete rabies-surveillance application in rural Kenya.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies tuberculosis contact investigation to screen contacts, identify active cases, quantify yield, and estimate missed cases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete contact-investigation application in São Paulo.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops an infection-prediction model and feature-importance analysis but does not explicitly conduct enhanced surveillance or case finding.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit lineage role for enhanced surveillance or case-finding tools.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a Melioidosis Checklist Index combining clinical, demographic, exposure, and lifestyle factors to aid early diagnosis and case reporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a new surveillance and case-reporting tool.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and implements a primary-care leptospirosis surveillance model combining passive and active case finding, clinical criteria, and rapid tests.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit surveillance-model development with an applied evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses record linkage and cross-validation between hospital and public-health records to measure notification completeness and underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit completeness assessment of communicable-disease reporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Links HIV cohorts to mortality records to quantify absent HIV/AIDS mentions and identify predictors of mortality underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit assessment of mortality-registry completeness.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares physician reports with electronic vital-registry linkage to measure death-ascertainment sensitivity and associated reporting biases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit comparison of manual and registry-based reporting completeness.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Independently validates reported CLABSI events through blinded chart review and quantifies underreporting and definition misinterpretation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit surveillance-reporting validation study.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares an electronic immunization registry with a household survey to assess coverage, dose underreporting, and duplicate records.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit registry completeness and quality assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a research cohort as the reference standard to assess completeness and accuracy of a routine HIV clinic database.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit data-quality validation against a reference database and medical records.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Measures tuberculosis underreporting in an epidemiological surveillance system and characterizes factors associated with missed cases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit assessment of surveillance underreporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares AIDS death certificates with notification-system records to quantify unreported AIDS cases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit cross-system assessment of reporting completeness.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Links tuberculosis and AIDS notification records to identify missing comorbidity information and quantify underreporting by state.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit linkage-based assessment of surveillance completeness.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares statutory measles notifications with health-insurance billing data to quantify reporting completeness during outbreak and sporadic periods.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit evaluation of mandated surveillance reporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Validates hospital CLABSI reports through blinded external review and adjudication, estimating sensitivity and corrected infection rates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit statewide surveillance-validation study.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Prospectively audits women after sutured perineal trauma to ascertain infections that would otherwise present across different care settings.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Title and follow-up audit explicitly investigate infection underreporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares two independent registries for septic arthritis after knee arthroscopy and demonstrates incomplete and inconsistent coding.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit cross-source agreement and completeness assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses record linkage between hospital diagnosis and infectious-disease surveillance sources to assess tuberculosis underreporting and diagnosis accuracy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit surveillance-quality and underreporting evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Quantifies misclassified, ill-defined, and unreported AIDS deaths and adjusts totals for mortality-system incompleteness.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit diagnosis and quantification of mortality-reporting defects.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Validates booster-vaccination records through parent questionnaires and recalculates coverage after accounting for unregistered vaccinations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit validation of vaccination-database completeness.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares clinically confirmed hepatitis infections with registry records and assesses completeness of case, demographic, and risk-factor reporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit surveillance-reporting completeness assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a community cluster survey and vaccination-card evidence to validate administratively reported yellow-fever vaccination coverage.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit independent validation of reported coverage.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs onsite interviews and chart reviews to validate CLABSI surveillance practices, reporting integrity, and case classification.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit validation of publicly reported surveillance data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares hospital, laboratory, and notification-system sources to quantify under-ascertainment of viral encephalitis and meningitis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit multisource surveillance-completeness assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses probabilistic linkage between notification and mortality databases to assess missing syphilis reports and associated perinatal deaths.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit cross-database underreporting assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares prescribed-drug-register exposure records with medical records to estimate indication-specific underreporting of palivizumab use.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit register-validity and completeness assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates uptake of a supplementary vaccination program but mentions under-reporting only as one possible explanation for low registry coverage without validating it.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit surveillance-data validation or completeness assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses hospital-data linkage to fill missing Indigenous status in communicable-disease notifications and measures the resulting completeness improvement.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application of linkage to improve notification completeness.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares routine antenatal HIV testing with sentinel surveillance against recommended standards, assessing agreement, errors, and completeness.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit evaluation of whether routine data can replace sentinel surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses adjusted denominators to address race misclassification while estimating hospitalization rates but does not evaluate surveillance-data completeness or validity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit validation or completeness assessment of an infectious-disease surveillance system.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares HIV prevalence from sentinel surveillance and program data at national, state, and district levels to evaluate program-data suitability.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit comparative evaluation of two HIV-surveillance data sources.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Links congenital-syphilis notification and mortality databases to identify unreported fetal and infant deaths.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit database-linkage assessment of underreporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates routine PMTCT testing data as a sentinel-surveillance alternative by comparing site results with a national reference laboratory.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit accuracy and quality assessment against a reference standard.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Validates reported surgical-site infections and denominator fields, quantifying missed infections and reporting errors.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit validation of surveillance data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses probabilistic linkage of tuberculosis and AIDS notification databases to estimate reciprocal underreporting and associated factors.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit linkage-based completeness evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Actively traces MDR-TB patients recorded as lost to follow-up and compares observed outcomes with registry classifications, revealing underreported favorable outcomes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit validation of recorded treatment outcomes.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares two syphilis information systems to identify condition- and year-specific underreporting among Indigenous people.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit cross-source reporting-completeness assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Probabilistically links a hepatitis C cohort with death-registry data to determine how often HCV was omitted from death certificates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit mortality-reporting completeness assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The title explicitly reports an assessment of underreported maternal hepatitis C status and its implications for infant testing.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit underreporting diagnosis in the title; no abstract was supplied.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Links laboratory and hospital-discharge data to measure pathogen-specific coding omissions and stool-sampling completeness.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit evaluation of underreporting and underdiagnosis in hospital and surveillance data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents a potential adjustment methodology for missing HIV-surveillance data and reporting delays, with model recommendations for harmonized use.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological source addressing surveillance-data defects.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Synthesizes state CLABSI validations, pools accuracy measures, and analyzes reasons for case misclassification and underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit benchmark of surveillance-reporting validity across jurisdictions.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates measles-reporting completeness by matching survey cases to notifications and independently comparing susceptible populations with reported cases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit dual-method completeness assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses probabilistic linkage of tuberculosis and AIDS notification systems to estimate TB underreporting and associated factors.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit linkage-based reporting-completeness assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares animal-bite patients identified at healthcare facilities with national surveillance totals and quantifies substantial underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit validation of surveillance counts against facility records.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares national invasive-bacterial-disease surveillance with hospital-discharge records to evaluate reporting completeness and interpret incidence trends.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit cross-source completeness assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Audits children recorded as overdue to estimate true vaccination coverage and identify upload errors, duplicates, and underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit audit of immunization-register accuracy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Matches clinically confirmed hepatitis cases with state surveillance records to estimate unreported proportions and associated characteristics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit surveillance-registry accuracy assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares mandatory syphilis surveillance with National Health Fund data and finds approximately twice as many cases in the latter.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit cross-source diagnosis of surveillance underreporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Pools state validation results for catheter-associated urinary-tract-infection reporting and assesses accuracy, error rates, and misclassification causes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit benchmark of surveillance-reporting validity.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Links hepatitis C laboratory surveillance with inpatient records and quantifies missing HCV coding for severe liver outcomes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit linked-data assessment of coding completeness.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Links hepatitis C laboratory reports to a transplant registry and explicitly assesses the degree of HCV underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit registry-completeness assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares single and linked administrative datasets and follow-up periods to quantify methodological underestimation of surgical-site-infection rates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit evaluation of ascertainment completeness across data designs.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Links drug-resistant-TB and ART registers to identify missing treatment status, dates, and deaths and quantify cross-register discrepancies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit data-quality and completeness assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducts repeated structured data-quality assessments using verification factors to measure surveillance-report accuracy and discrepancies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit routine diagnostic assessment of integrated surveillance data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the WHO Data Quality Self-assessment tool to compare immunization-register tallies with submitted reports and quantify discrepancies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit routine-report accuracy assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Validates vaccination-register coverage through parent questionnaires and documentation, then calculates corrected coverage.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit vaccination-register validation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Assesses completeness of Zika surveillance reports using burden measures that expose implausible values and likely severe underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit assessment of infectious-disease surveillance reports.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares malaria records at health centers and district offices to identify uncaptured cases and assess reporting quality.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit evaluation of surveillance-reporting quality.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Links facility records with two national tuberculosis systems and assesses underreporting, timeliness, concordance, accuracy, and completeness.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit multidimensional surveillance-quality assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares financial-reporting sources for immunization spending, not infectious-disease surveillance data or case-reporting completeness.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No lineage role for surveillance data validation and completeness assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Assesses completeness of HIV testing and status fields in Armenia's national tuberculosis database and relates missingness to outcomes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit assessment of national program-data completeness.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Matches prospectively confirmed periprosthetic joint infections to registry records to validate reporting and estimate missed national burden.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit registry-reporting validation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies an enhanced claims-based identification algorithm and registry matching to improve enumeration of transgender people with HIV.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to address undercounting in New York City HIV data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares immunization tallies, facility reports, and DHIS2 records and quantifies normal, under-, and over-reporting accuracy ratios.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit routine immunization-data accuracy assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Assesses non-publication of sepsis clinical-trial results rather than completeness or validity of infectious-disease surveillance data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The reporting problem concerns trial dissemination, not surveillance measurement.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares written animal-exposure reports with two electronic systems to identify missing rabies-exposure and prophylaxis records.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit cross-system reporting-gap assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares antimicrobial-use measures from two cattle registries using correlation and agreement analyses and identifies systematic underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit comparative assessment of recorded-data accuracy and completeness.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares molecularly identified Coxiella burnetii infections with national surveillance channels and quantifies unreported cases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit case-reporting completeness assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Links death certificates, viral-hepatitis diagnoses, and severe-liver-disease admissions to evaluate mortality underreporting and its effect on national estimates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit linked-data sensitivity analysis of mortality-reporting completeness.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Independently estimates HIV prevalence among pregnant women and compares detection with the notification system, identifying likely missed cases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit independent assessment suggesting surveillance underreporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares tuberculosis patterns in North Korean defectors with official North Korean burden reports and evaluates discrepancies indicating possible underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit, though indirect, assessment of official burden-report plausibility.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares documented HIV-related deaths in medical records with official cause-of-death data and quantifies omitted HIV/AIDS attribution.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit mortality-reporting completeness assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Links two pregnancy-vaccination sources and assesses coverage, agreement, and underreporting by year and care characteristics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit cross-source reporting validation and completeness assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Directly examines how culture-independent diagnostics threaten or support enteric surveillance objectives and recommends validation and subtyping-compatible strategies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit surveillance guidance addressing a changing diagnostic design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops variance-based statistical approaches for selecting biochemical markers that improve strain definition and outbreak-detection algorithms.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development for surveillance-based outbreak detection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Derives a maximum-likelihood estimator of disease-episode duration from consecutive cross-sectional observations and evaluates it by simulation and example.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of an epidemiological measurement design applicable to infectious-disease surveys.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a food-chain simulation model to investigate surveillance designs combining genomic and high-fidelity epidemiological metadata.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological exploration of an integrated food-safety surveillance design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a novel simulation model to compare the sensitivity and specificity of alternative viral-load monitoring schedules during pregnancy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit performance evaluation of alternative monitoring designs.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulates correlated infection patterns across prevalence and batch-size scenarios to evaluate the efficiency limits of specimen pooling.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit performance evaluation of a testing design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Surveys national automated laboratory-reporting systems, documenting their surveillance uses, validation status, coverage variation, and implementation barriers.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Provides explicit system-level findings and recommendations for automated surveillance reporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies targeted testing, random surveillance screening, variant surveillance, contact tracing, and related benchmarks in a large workplace.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete workplace application of multiple SARS-CoV-2 surveillance designs.
